texto: Then the truth began to reveal itself, as it always does. truth seeks the light of day,needs it just like we need air, and so it finds ways to seep out of the sturdiest. most skillfully hidden boxes--even those buried deeply in the hearts of the dead.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Wendy Webb: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “Then the truth began to reveal itself, as it always does. truth seeks the light of day,needs it j…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, secrets, verdade pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de verdade.

frase_original: Then the truth began to reveal itself, as it always does. truth seeks the light of day,needs it just like we need air, and so it finds ways to seep out of the sturdiest. most skillfully hidden boxes--even those buried deeply in the hearts of the dead.
